response.justification: Bogdan presents a genuinely strong distributed-systems background — the Dunnhumby role (millions of HTTP requests per second, async I/O, low-latency microservices) maps well to the high-throughput architecture requirement, and his current Acme Software position involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, which is directly aligned with the AI-native engineering environment. However, the single most disqualifying gap is the complete absence of Python anywhere in his resume or skills list, which is an explicitly stated must-have for the role. His TypeScript depth is solid, his staff-level pedigree is credible, and his breadth across systems is real — but without any Python signal, a structured interview would need to establish whether this is a genuine gap or simply an omission before advancing him further.
strengths: Hands-on distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O — directly maps to high-throughput architecture requirements
Current role involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators, demonstrating real engagement with AI-native development tooling
Repeated staff-level designations across multiple employers, open-source platform ownership, and public talks indicate genuine technical leadership beyond IC execution
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the resume — skills list, job descriptions, and project descriptions mention no Python whatsoever, directly contradicting the must-have 'Strong Python' requirement

No explicit evidence of stakeholder management, cross-functional coordination, or bridging engineering and product perspectives — the resume reads as purely technical IC with no visibility into strategic influence
The AI-native work at current employer began April 2025 (~1 month tenure at time of resume), making it too early to assess depth or impact in that context
